questions.question: A circle has center at (3, –2) and passes through the point (7, 1). Write the equation of the circle.
questions.explanation: Find the radius using the distance formula: r = √[(7-3)² + (1-(-2))²] = √[16 + 9] = √25 = 5. The standard form is (x - h)² + (y - k)² = r².

questions.question: A sector of a circle with radius 8 has a central angle of 45°. What is the arc length of this sector? (Leave your answer in terms of π.)
A line is tangent to a circle with center O at point P. If OP = 6, what is the relationship between OP and the tangent line at P?
questions.answer: OP is perpendicular to the tangent line
questions.explanation: A radius drawn to a point of tangency is always perpendicular to the tangent line at that point. This is a fundamental property of circles.

questions.question: A circle has equation x² + y² – 6x + 4y – 12 = 0. Find the radius of this circle.
questions.explanation: Complete the square: (x² - 6x + 9) + (y² + 4y + 4) = 12 + 9 + 4 = 25. So (x - 3)² + (y + 2)² = 25, which gives r² = 25, so r = 5.

questions.question: A sector of a circle with radius 10 has an area of 25π. What is the central angle of the sector in degrees?
Two concentric circles have radii 4 and 7. What is the area of the ring (annulus) between them?
questions.explanation: Area of annulus = π(R²) – π(r²) = π(7²) – π(4²) = 49π – 16π = 33π.
